title: dysoxylum richii
intro: Dysoxylum richii, also known as the Philippine pyinkado or Philippine pyinkado tree, is a species of plant in the family Meliaceae, native to the Philippines. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and triterpenoids. The health benefits of Dysoxylum richii include the potential treatment of various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as its use as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. Dysoxylum richii can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional Filipino medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
